japanese_name: ビオレ 毛穴すっきりパック 鼻用 黒色タイプ
description: Bioré nose pore strips. Pulls the drama out of your pores in one satisfying rip, best grabbed at a drugstore before mirror time gets personal.

english_name: OS-1 Oral Rehydration Solution Drink and Jelly
japanese_name: オーエスワン 経口補水液 / オーエスワンゼリー
description: OS-1 drink and jelly. Salty-sweet rehydration for real dehydration, not casual sipping. Drugstore pick for heat, fever, or the post-onsen flop.

japanese_name: セブン-イレブン おでん
description: Konbini oden. Hot daikon, egg, tofu, and fishcake sitting in broth like winter has a staffing department. Grab it from the counter when Japan gets cold and dramatic.
notes: [VENUE_CATEGORY: 7-Eleven hot case; daikon; egg; shirataki; chikuwa]

japanese_name: 太田胃散 分包
description: Ohta's Isan stomach powder. Herbal, chalky, and very much not candy. Locals reach for it after ramen, booze, or the bold decision to eat everything.

japanese_name: 南風堂 ちんすこう
description: Chinsuko cookies. Okinawan shortbread with a sandy little crumble that tastes better than that description sounds. Easy suitcase snack, dangerously easy to finish.

japanese_name: 御菓子御殿 元祖紅いもタルト
description: Beni-imo tart. Purple sweet potato in a tiny pastry shell, because Okinawa looked at dessert and chose elegance with carbs. Airport souvenir classic.

japanese_name: 南風堂 ちんすこう
description: Okinawan chinsuko cookies. Buttery little shortbread bricks for people who say they are buying souvenirs, then eat one bag at the hotel.

japanese_name: 明治神宮 お守り
description: Omamori charm. A small shrine amulet for wishes like safe travel, good health, or emotional damage control. Buy one at the shrine, keep it respectfully.

japanese_name: 明治神宮 おみくじ・絵馬
description: Omikuji and ema. Pull a fortune, write a wish, and let a shrine tree hold your emotional paperwork. Cheap, meaningful, and very Japan-coded.

japanese_name: セブン-イレブン ツナマヨネーズおにぎり
description: Tuna mayo onigiri. Rice ball, crisp seaweed, creamy tuna center. The konbini meal that somehow fixes 2 a.m. and train-platform hunger.
notes: [VENUE_CATEGORY: 7-Eleven; tuna mayonnaise onigiri; salmon onigiri; kombu onigiri]

japanese_name: オペラ リップティントN
description: Opera Lip Tint. Glossy color that survives better than most travel plans. Loft and Plaza staple for a quick drugstore beauty grab.

japanese_name: オーラツーミー マウススプレー
description: Ora2 mouth spray. Tiny breath reset for trains, dates, and garlic situations with consequences. Lives near the toothpaste at Japanese drugstores.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Ora2 me Mouth Spray; Ora2 me Mouthwash; Ora2 me Travel Set]

japanese_name: ダイソー 和風ちよがみ
description: Daiso chiyogami paper. Tiny patterned paper squares that make your luggage look cultured for ¥110. Easy gift, zero suitcase drama.

japanese_name: 551蓬莱 豚まん
description: 551 Horai pork buns. Osaka's steamed pork bun with a cult-level train-station smell. Buy hot, eat fast, regret nothing.

input_name: Otsumami Konbini Bar Snacks (Atari-Me, Kaki Pi, Saki-Ika)
japanese_name: 亀田の柿の種 6袋詰
description: Kaki no Tane. Crunchy rice crackers and peanuts that make konbini drinking feel suddenly organized. The default beer snack when dinner is snacks in a bag.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: atari-me; kaki pi; saki-ika; mixed nuts]

japanese_name: ドンキ 大容量お菓子パック
description: Oversized snack packs. Japan looks at normal snack size and says, 'coward.' Donki stacks these big souvenir bags for people buying candy like they owe apologies.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: big KitKat bags; giant Pocky; regional Pretz; assorted senbei packs]

japanese_name: パブロンゴールドA
description: Pabron Gold A. Multi-symptom cold medicine for the 'my nose is filing taxes without me' phase. Ask the pharmacist first, especially if you take other meds.
notes: [SOURCE_LIMITED: official product page not found; verified via Taisho notice and retailer listings]

japanese_name: パブロンSゴールドW錠
description: Pabron S Gold W. Multi-symptom cold tablets for cough, sore throat, runny nose, and full-body 'I'm done.' Drugstore counter pick when tissues become your personality.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: Pabron S Gold W; Pabron Gold A; Pabron Ace Pro-X]

japanese_name: パンシロンキュアSP錠
description: Panshiron Cure. Stomach tablets for heartburn, acid reflux, and the ramen bowl that fought back. Drugstore rescue after rich food goes rogue.

japanese_name: PASMO PASSPORT
description: PASMO PASSPORT. Tourist IC card for trains and small payments, now retired like a limited-edition transit ghost. Look for TOURIST PASMO instead.

japanese_name: ぺんてる エナージェル
description: Pentel EnerGel. Smooth gel pen that makes hotel-note chaos look intentional. Stationery people buy one, then accidentally become pen people.

japanese_name: ダイソー プチブロック 大人ペンギン
description: Daiso Petit Block. Tiny block kits for ¥110 that somehow demand focus and emotional investment. Great hotel-night project when your feet quit.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: Adult Penguin; Shiba Inu; Giant Panda; Tyrannosaurus]

japanese_name: ダイソー 折りたたみ式スマホスタンド
description: Daiso folding phone stand. ¥110 plastic that saves your neck from hotel-bed doomscroll posture. Buy one before watching maps, menus, and regret for hours.

english_name: Pigeon Oshiri Nap Premium Thick Baby Wipes
japanese_name: ピジョン おしりナップ プレミアム極上厚手
description: Pigeon baby wipes. Soft, thick wipes for diaper changes, sticky hands, and travel messes with main-character timing. Parenting aisle staple in Japan.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: Pigeon Oshiri Nap wipes; Pigeon Baby Cream 50g; Pigeon Baby Powder]

english_name: Pilot FriXion Ball Knock Erasable Pen
japanese_name: パイロット フリクションボールノック
description: Pilot FriXion pen. Writes like a normal gel pen, erases with friction, and quietly fixes your mistakes. Stationery-shop classic for planners and overthinkers.
